ctxMarket: Attention is on Solana and Jupiter after notable rallies and surging trading volumes. Institutional interest is highlighted by new ETF filings and growing Solana treasury holdings among public companies.
ctxTalking:
• Jupiter’s 14% price rally and a 113% volume spike, with traders increasing leveraged positions
• Morgan Stanley’s proposed 0.14% annual fee for Solana and Ethereum ETFs, the lowest globally for these products
• Ongoing debate about whether Solana’s recent rally is driven by genuine demand or hype
ctxMatters:
ETF fee competition could influence how institutions allocate to Solana versus other assets.  
Increased leveraged trading and treasury accumulation signal growing confidence and risk appetite among both retail and institutional participants.  
Speculation around the “shilling” narrative reflects ongoing scrutiny of Solana’s price drivers.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Institutional ETF filings and treasury holdings point to sustained interest in Solana’s ecosystem.
Noise: Social media debates about “shilling” lack clear data support.
ctxGlossary:
ETF — Exchange-traded fund, a regulated investment product tracking an asset or basket of assets  
Treasury — Crypto assets held on the balance sheets of public companies

readMore.title: Morgan Stanley’s proposed 0.14% ETH and SOL fees could turn the next crypto ETF race into a price fight
The 5 Largest Publicly Traded Solana Treasury Firms
